content_markdown:
A sound wave travels through a gas at a speed of $270\text{ m s}^{-1}$. The graph shows the variation of the displacement $s$ of the gas particles with distance $d$ from the source.

What is the frequency of the wave?

diagram_metadata.description: A sinusoidal graph showing displacement s in mm on the y-axis against distance d in m on the x-axis. The wave starts at the origin, reaches a peak at 0.375 m, crosses the x-axis at 0.75 m, reaches a trough at 1.125 m, and completes one full cycle at 1.5 m.

vibe_explanation: From the graph, we can determine the wavelength $\lambda$ by looking at the distance for one complete cycle. The wave returns to the same phase at $d = 1.5\text{ m}$, so $\lambda = 1.5\text{ m}$. Using the wave equation $v = f\lambda$, we can solve for frequency: $f = \frac{v}{\lambda} = \frac{270}{1.5} = 180\text{ Hz}$.
